scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE PRINCIPAL JUDGE, CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

O.S. NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

BETWEEN:

SMT. ANITA SHARMA, Aged 52 years, {{OCCUPATION}}, Resident of Indiranagar, Bengaluru, {{PIN_CODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

MR. RAJESH REDDY, Aged 60 years, {{OCCUPATION}}, Resident of HSR Layout, Bengaluru, {{PIN_CODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF AGREEMENT TO SELL

FACTS

1. The Plaintiff states that she entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant dated 10.01.2024 for the purchase of a residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft.

2. The Plaintiff states that the total consideration agreed upon for the suit property is Rs. 85,00,000/-. On the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654.

3. The Plaintiff states that as per the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

4. The Plaintiff states that she has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has arranged the necessary funds to pay the balance consideration and was prepared to execute the Sale Deed on the stipulated date.

5. The Plaintiff states that on 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the Sale Deed. The Defendant claimed in said letter that he had received a higher offer for the suit property from a third party.

6. The Plaintiff states that she issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to honour the contract and execute the Sale Deed as agreed. The notice was sent via post and the postal receipt is preserved.

7. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant refused to comply with the legal notice and has consistently failed to execute the Sale Deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness to perform her obligations.

8. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant's refusal constitutes a breach of the contract, entitling the Plaintiff to seek specific performance of the Agreement to Sell.

LEGAL_BASIS

9. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 10 (specific performance shall be enforced subject to Sections 11(2), 14, and 16) Specific Relief Act, 1963.

10. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 16 Specific Relief Act, 1963 regarding readiness and willingness.

11. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 21 Specific Relief Act, 1963 regarding compensation.

12. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 22 Specific Relief Act, 1963.

13. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 10 Indian Contract Act, 1872 regarding valid contract.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 16 Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 regarding jurisdiction over immovable property.

15. The Plaintiff relies upon Article 54 Limitation Act, 1963 regarding limitation period.

16. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 53A Transfer of Property Act, 1882.

17. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 17(1A) Registration Act, 1908.

CAUSE_OF_ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 at Bengaluru when the Defendant, by his letter, refused/repudiated his obligation to execute and register the sale deed under the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.

19. The cause of action further/alternatively arose on 10.07.2024, being the date fixed for performance under the Agreement to Sell, when the Defendant failed and neglected to perform his contractual obligation to execute and register the sale deed against receipt of the balance consideration.

20. The cause of action is continuing and subsists, including upon the Plaintiff’s demand made by legal notice dated 20.06.2024 and the Defendant’s continued refusal/failure to execute and register the sale deed.

21. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court as the suit property is situated in Bengaluru and the Defendant resides in Bengaluru.

LIMITATION

22. The suit is governed by Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963.

23. The period of limitation is three years from the date fixed for performance.

24. The present suit is filed within the prescribed period of limitation.

JURISDICTION

25. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the suit property is situated within Bengaluru and the Defendant resides within Bengaluru.

26. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the suit based on the valuation of the suit property.

27. The jurisdiction is invoked under Section 16 Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

VALUATION_COURT_FEE

28. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- for the purpose of jurisdiction.

29. The court fee payable is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

30. The requisite court fee has been paid/is to be paid on the plaint.

INTEREST

31. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest if permitted by contract, usage, or substantive law.

32. The Plaintiff claims pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 as this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

SCHEDULE_OF_PROPERTY

33. Residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru.

34. Extent/Area: 1200 sq ft.

35. Boundaries (to be populated as per Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 / Annexure P-1 before filing):
   East: {{EAST_BOUNDARY}}
   West: {{WEST_BOUNDARY}}
   North: {{NORTH_BOUNDARY}}
   South: {{SOUTH_BOUNDARY}}

PRAYER

(a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-

(b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession)

(c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit

(d) Award pre-suit interest if contract, usage, or substantive law permits, and pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 CPC as this Hon'ble Court deems fit

(e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff

(f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper

DOCUMENTS_LIST

36. Agreement to Sell (original) dated 10.01.2024 - Annexure P-1

37. RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654) - Annexure P-2

38. Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024 - Annexure P-3

39. Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt - Annexure P-4

VERIFICATION

I, Smt. Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 8 are true to my personal knowledge, and the contents of paragraphs 9 to 39 are based on records, legal advice, and information which I believe to be true.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_FILING}}.

SIGNATURE OF PLAINTIFF

(SMT. ANITA SHARMA)

{{ADVOCATE_DETAILS}}

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF
Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COMMERCIAL COURT AT BENGALURU
ORIGINAL SUIT NUMBER: {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF 2024

COURT_HEADING

PRINCIPAL JUDGE, COMMERCIAL COURT, BENGALURU

PARTIES

PLAINTIFF:

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD,

A COMPANY REGISTERED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT,
HAVING ITS REGISTERED OFFICE AT MG ROAD, BENGALURU, {{PIN CODE}}

REPRESENTED BY ITS DIRECTOR PRIYA MENON, AGED 40 YEARS.

VERSUS

DEFENDANT:

CLOUDBRIDGE SYSTEMS LLP,

A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP,
HAVING ITS OFFICE AT WHITEFIELD, BENGALURU, {{PIN CODE}}

REPRESENTED BY ITS DESIGNATED PARTNER VIKRAM SINGH, AGED 48 YEARS.

SUIT_TITLE

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

FACTS

1. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, engaged in the business of software solutions. The Plaintiff is represented by its Director, Priya Menon, who is authorized to file this plaint.

2. The Defendant is a limited liability partnership engaged in software development services. The Defendant is represented by its Designated Partner, Vikram Singh.

3. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system. The total contract value was agreed at Rs.45,00,000/-.

4. Pursuant to the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid Rs.20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT. The transaction bears UTR Number ICIC20240201456789.

5. Under the terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

6. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

7. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project entirely without completing the deliverables.

8. The Defendant has failed to return the advance payment of Rs.20,00,000/- despite the abandonment of the project.

9. Due to the non-delivery and abandonment, the Plaintiff suffered additional loss of Rs.12,00,000/- on account of delay in operations. This loss has been assessed by an auditor.

10. The total damages suffered by the Plaintiff amount to Rs.32,00,000/-, comprising Rs.20,00,000/- as refund of advance and Rs.12,00,000/- as consequential damages.

11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the amount and compensate for the losses.

12. The Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice and did not effect any settlement.

13. The Plaintiff has complied with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 by undertaking pre-institution mediation in respect of the present commercial dispute, and has received a non-starter/failed settlement certificate/report dated {{MEDIATION_CERT_DATE}} bearing No. {{MEDIATION_CERT_NO}} (and Case/Reference No. {{MEDIATION_CASE_NO}}) issued by {{MEDIATION_AUTHORITY}}. The Plaintiff is filing the present suit upon such certificate/report.

14. The cause of action first accrued on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the contractual deadline, further accrued on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project, and continues thereafter due to the Defendant’s failure to refund the advance and compensate the Plaintiff’s losses.

15. The suit is within the period of limitation.

LEGAL_BASIS

16. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 for compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract.

17. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 regarding jurisdiction where the cause of action arose.

18. The Plaintiff relies upon Article 55 of the Limitation Act, 1963 regarding the period of limitation for suits relating to contracts.

19. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 regarding interest on decrees.

20. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 35 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 regarding costs of the suit.

CAUSE_OF_ACTION

21. The cause of action arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the contractual deadline, and further on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and failed to perform its contractual obligations.

22. The cause of action arose within the territorial limits of Bengaluru, where the contract was performed and the Plaintiff's office is located.

23. The cause of action is continuing due to the failure to refund the advance and pay damages.

LIMITATION

24. The suit is governed by Article 55 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

25. The prescribed period is three years from the date the contract was broken.

26. The contract was broken at least on 01.05.2024 (on failure to deliver by the agreed deadline), and in any event on 15.06.2024 (on abandonment). The suit is filed within three years from the date of such breach.

JURISDICTION

27. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as per Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, since the cause of action arose wholly within Bengaluru.

28. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit exceeds the specified value under the Commercial Courts Act.

29. The suit is filed as a commercial dispute under the Commercial Courts Act.

VALUATION_COURT_FEE

30. The suit is valued at Rs.32,00,000/- for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee.

31. Court fee of Rs.{{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on the plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

INTEREST

32. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at the rate of 18% per annum from 15.06.2024 till the date of filing of the suit, based on the contractual obligation and substantive law.

33. The Plaintiff claims pendente lite interest and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

PRAYER

The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree as follows:

(a) decree for damages in the sum of Rs.32,00,000/- on account of breach of contract (S.73 ICA)

(b) pre-suit interest at the rate of 18% per annum from 15.06.2024 till date of filing (Contract / usage / substantive law)

(c) pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from filing till decree (S.34 CPC)

(d) future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from decree till realisation (S.34 CPC)

(e) costs of the suit including advocate's fees (S.35 CPC)

(f) such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper

DOCUMENTS_LIST

34. Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024 (Annexure P-1)

35. NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) dated 01.02.2024 (Annexure P-2)

36. Email correspondence showing abandonment (Annexure P-3)

37. Loss assessment report by auditor (Annexure P-4)

38. Legal notice dated 01.07.2024 (Annexure P-5)

VERIFICATION

I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare that what is stated in paragraphs 1 to 10 of the Facts section is within my personal knowledge and what is stated in paragraphs 11 to 15 is based on information derived from the records of the company and legal advice, which I believe to be true.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_FILING}} day of {{MONTH}},

2024.

STATEMENT OF TRUTH (Commercial Dispute)

I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby state that I have read and understood the contents of the plaint and the accompanying documents, and that the statements made therein are true and correct to my knowledge, and where based on information, I believe the same to be true, and that no material facts have been concealed therefrom.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_FILING}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

SIGNATURE

PRIYA MENON

DIRECTOR

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD

PLACE: BENGALURU
DATE: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

ADVOCATE BLOCK

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADDRESS}}
{{BAR_COUNCIL_NUMBER}}

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF
Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
